articles.title: Heatwave tightens grip on Karnataka: 42°C likely in North, Bengaluru stays dry and hot - newsfirstprime.com
articles.full_text:
Severe summer conditions continue across the state, with heatwave warnings in North Karnataka, rising humidity along the coast, and light rain expected in Malnad regions.
Karnataka is set to witness continued intense summer conditions, with temperatures rising sharply across several regions. The India Meteorological Department has warned that the current heatwave is likely to persist, especially affecting North Karnataka, while other parts of the state will experience varied weather patterns.
In Bengaluru, residents can expect dry weather conditions with temperatures ranging between 35°C and 37°C. The city will continue to experience warm days without significant relief in the coming days.
The situation is more severe in North Karnataka, where districts like Kalaburagi are likely to record extremely high temperatures, touching up to 42°C. The weather department has issued a heatwave alert for the region, cautioning people about the risks associated with prolonged exposure to high temperatures.

Authorities have specifically advised residents in North Karnataka to avoid stepping out between 12 noon and 4 PM, as hot winds during these hours could pose serious health risks.
Meanwhile, coastal Karnataka is witnessing a different trend. Districts such as Mangaluru and Udupi are experiencing increased humidity levels, making the weather feel hotter and more uncomfortable despite slightly lower temperatures.
In contrast, the Malnad region is expected to see relatively cooler conditions. Areas in Shivamogga and Chikkamagaluru districts are likely to experience cloudy skies, with chances of light drizzle in some places. Night temperatures in these regions are expected to remain between 20°C and 22°C, offering some relief compared to other parts of the state.
